canonical_definition: The prevailing usage of authorship attribution in the provided literature is as a behavior involving the classification of a text's origin, most commonly as a binary distinction between human-written and machine-generated content. This task appears under several names, including 'LLM text detection', 'AI-generated text detection', and 'Machine-generated text detection', with one paper noting that methods have "traditionally formulated the task as a binary classification problem" (CAT: Causal Attention Tuning For Injecting Fine-grained Causal Knowledge into Large Language Models). A smaller set of papers defines more specific tasks, such as determining if two texts share the same author, verifying if a text was written by a specific known author ('Authorship verification'), or identifying which particular LLM generated a text ('Text origin detection'). This behavior is operationalized and measured using benchmarks like the MAGE dataset, on which models are trained and evaluated. The technical approaches mentioned for this classification include leveraging features from logit distributions or from "entity graphs and contextual representations" (T2: An Adaptive Test-Time Scaling Strategy for Contextual Question Answering). The literature also discusses related but distinct behaviors, such as 'Authorship mimicking' (replicating a style) and 'Authorship obfuscation' (concealing a style). The ambiguity this task seeks to resolve is linked to the concept of 'Accountability', which is described as being diffused when authorship is unclear, a phenomenon quantified by one paper as 'Authorship entropy'.

Spontaneous Giving and Calculated Greed in Language Models

definitions.text: The task of classifying a given piece of text as either written by a human or generated by a large language model.
definitions.paper: CAT: Causal Attention Tuning For Injecting Fine-grained Causal Knowledge into Large Language Models

definitions.text: The observable task of taking two collections of text and producing a score indicating the likelihood they were written by the same author.
definitions.paper: CoBA: Counterbias Text Augmentation for Mitigating Various Spurious Correlations via Semantic Triples

definitions.text: Performing binary classification to distinguish between human-written and machine-generated texts based on features derived from entity graphs and contextual representations.
definitions.paper: T2: An Adaptive Test-Time Scaling Strategy for Contextual Question Answering

definitions.text: The observable task of classifying a given text as originating from an AI model or a human.
definitions.paper: Spontaneous Giving and Calculated Greed in Language Models

definitions.text: The observable task of determining whether a given piece of text was written by a specific, known author based on stylistic evidence.
definitions.paper: RETAIL: Towards Real-world Travel Planning for Large Language Models

definitions.text: The observable task of determining which specific LLM generated a given piece of text, based on analysis of its statistical or representational patterns.
definitions.paper: Social Genome: Grounded Social Reasoning Abilities of Multimodal Models

definitions.text: Classifying a text as human-written or AI-generated based on its surface and distributional properties.
The task of classifying a given text as either generated by a large language model or written by a human.
definitions.paper: MUZO: Leveraging Multiple Queries and Momentum for Zeroth-Order Fine-Tuning of Large Language Models

definitions.text: The observable task of generating text that replicates the distinctive writing style of a specific target author.
definitions.paper: RETAIL: Towards Real-world Travel Planning for Large Language Models

definitions.text: The observable task of altering the stylistic features of a text to conceal the original author's identity while preserving the semantic content.
definitions.paper: RETAIL: Towards Real-world Travel Planning for Large Language Models

definitions.text: The latent ability of a model or system to distinguish between human-authored and machine-generated text based on underlying linguistic or statistical patterns.
The degree to which an author can be held responsible for the content and consequences of a text, a norm which is diffused when authorship is ambiguous.
definitions.paper: Refining Text Generation for Realistic Conversational Recommendation via Direct Preference Optimization

definitions.text: A measure of uncertainty regarding the cognitive origin of a text, quantifying the diffusion of accountability when it is unclear whether a text was authored by a human, an LLM, or through hybrid collaboration.
definitions.paper: Refining Text Generation for Realistic Conversational Recommendation via Direct Preference Optimization

relationships.paper: MetaFaith: Faithful Natural Language Uncertainty Expression inLLMs
relationships.evidence: For the machine-generated text detector, we trained and evaluated three pretrained models (RoBERTa, DeBERTav3, and ModernBERT) on the MAGE dataset (Li et al., 2024), a machine-generated text detection benchmark based on documents from 10 domains.

emnlp25: MetaFaith: Faithful Natural Language Uncertainty Expression inLLMs

relationships.paper: CoBA: Counterbias Text Augmentation for Mitigating Various Spurious Correlations via Semantic Triples
relationships.evidence: our ablation study evaluates the impact of different layers in the LIGHT-LUAR model by systematically removing each layer from the aggregation and measuring its effect on attribution performance across three datasets (Section 6.2.1)

emnlp25: CoBA: Counterbias Text Augmentation for Mitigating Various Spurious Correlations via Semantic Triples
